title: gap
description: The gap property sets the spacing between flex or grid items. It applies to the container — not to the children — making it easy to space items evenly without margin hacks.
challenge: Add spacing between the flex items. Use gap on the container — no margins on children.
realWorldUsage: In component libraries, gap has replaced most margin-on-children patterns. The key principle is that spacing belongs in the container, not the children — components without built-in margins can be dropped into any layout and spaced however the parent needs, with no overrides required.
